data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Friday: CRCL, CAG, MANU and NVO
data.description:
U.S. stock index futures on Friday surged after a strong May jobs report. Here are some stocks to watch on Friday: 

  • Shares of Circle Internet (NYSE:CRCL) were +15.3% in pre-market trading, a day after the stock saw a blockbuster debut on the New York Stock Exchange. The stablecoin issuer raised nearly $1.1B in an upsized initial public offering (IPO). Its shares opened at $69 on Thursday, more than double the IPO price of $31 per share and valuing the stock at nearly $18B. Circle's (NYSE:CRCL) debut is a shot in the arm for an IPO market that has been struggling.
  • Conagra Brands (NYSE:CAG) stock was +0.3% ahead of the opening bell. The consumer foods company said it would sell its Van de Kamp's and Mrs. Paul's frozen seafood brands for $55M in cash to High Liner Foods. The products from those brands that were a part of the deal contributed about $75M to Conagra's (NYSE:CAG) 2024 net sales. "This divestiture allows us to further focus our efforts on strengthening our core frozen offerings," CEO Sean Connolly said.
  • Class A shares of Manchester United (NYSE:MANU) were +5.4% ahead of market open, after the famed soccer club raised its fiscal full year 2025 adjusted EBITDA guidance to £180M-£190M from a prior range of £145M-£160M. The company's men's team had a strong Europa League, reaching the finals where they lost to Tottenham. However, the team had its worst Premier League season since they were relegated in 1974, finishing in 15th position. 
  • U.S.-listed shares of Novo Nordisk (NVO) were in focus, +1.9% in pre-market trading. Europe's drug regulator on Friday said that its safety committee had concluded that Novo’s (NVO) blockbuster weight loss/diabetes therapy semaglutide could cause a side effect in the eyes that could lead to vision loss in very rare cases. The committee recommended that semaglutide's product information be revised to reflect its potential to cause the condition.

data.title: Elon Musk's xAI expected to generate $13B in annual earnings by 2029: report
data.description:
Elon Musk's generative artificial intelligence startup xAI is expected to generate $13B in annual earnings by 2029, Bloomberg reported, citing documents by its banker, Morgan Stanley.

Founded in March 2023 and publicly unveiled to the world a few months later, xAI generated $52M in gross revenue in the first-quarter and lost $341M in EBITDA, the news outlet added, citing people familiar with the bank's presentation.

The widely watched metric by investors is expected to hit $2.7B in 2027 and $13.1B in 2029, one of the people added.

Gross revenue is expected to hit $1B this year and $14B by 2029, the person added. Cash flow from operations was a negative $220M, following $2.6B in capital expenditures, of which the majority was on data centers. Data center spending is expected to hit $18B going forward.

Musk's xAI did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Musk wanted to put a “proper value” on xAI, according to media reports in April. Maker of the Grok chatbot, xAI raised $6B in new funding late last year that valued the company at $50B. Some of that funding went towards xAI's massive data center in Memphis, where it used 100,000 of Nvidia's (NVDA) H100 GPUs.

In March, xAI acquired Musk's social network X in an all-stock deal. The move propelled xAI to a $113B valuation.

Earlier this week, it was reported that xAI would hold a $300M share sale for new investors.

Musk, who also leads SpaceX (SPACE) and Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA), said in July 2024 he would bring the idea of Tesla investing $5B into xAI to the electric vehicle maker's board of directors after the idea received nearly two-thirds of “yes” votes in an online poll.

data.title: Torrid Holdings to close 180 stores, trims FY25 guidance
data.description:
Shares of Torrid Holdings (NYSE:CURV) are trading defensively in Friday’s premarket as the company’s lowered sales guidance for FY25 reflects tariff mitigation efforts and plans to close up to 180 underperforming stores this year.

For the quarter ended May 3, Torrid (NYSE:CURV) earned a profit of $0.06 per share, half of what the company earned last year but 2 cents better than expected, while revenue dropped 5% to a below-consensus $266M. Due to promotional activity and corporate investments, gross profit margin narrowed by 320 basis points to 38.1%.

Comparable sales were down 4% during the quarter versus -9% in the same quarter last year.

Looking ahead to FY25, as the company accelerates its transformation to a more digitally led business, it plans to shutter stores and reinvest in areas to drive “long-term growth.”

This includes investing in sub-brands to attract new and younger customers, reactivating lapsed customers, and “creating a halo effect for the mainline Torrid offerings,” CEO Lisa Harper said on the company’s earnings call.

The company now expects sales to be between $1.03B to $1.055B versus earlier guidance of $1.08B to $1.10B, the midpoint of which is above the $1.01B estimate.

Adjusted EBITDA guidance was also lowered to $95M to $105M from $100M to $110M, previously.

For Q2, Torrid (NYSE:CURV) forecasts net sales to be between $250M to $265M vs $279.44M expectations.

data.title: iRobot surges on further extension to waiver on obligations
data.description:
iRobot (NASDAQ:IRBT) stock surged on Friday after the consumer robot maker disclosed a further extension to the waiver on covenant obligations by the lenders of its credit agreement.

Shares were +4.18% Friday pre-market to $3.49.

As part of the latest amendment, the lenders have waived until August 14, 2025, the company's covenant obligations to provide a report and opinion of its auditor concerning its annual financial statements and maintain a minimum level of core assets.

In connection with the latest amendment, the company is required to make a $4M prepayment to the lenders. Additionally, the company issued warrants to the lenders to purchase an aggregate of 1.56M shares of common stock.

"The waiver of events of default under our senior secured term loan credit facility is time-limited; if this waiver is not extended at the end of the applicable period, we will be in default," said the company.

"Our senior secured term loan credit facility provides our lenders with a first-priority lien against substantially all of our assets, and contains financial covenants and other restrictions on our actions that may limit our operational flexibility or otherwise adversely affect our results of operations," added the company.

data.title: Braze falls on underwhelming Q1 revenue, analysts optimistic on OfferFit acquisition
data.description:
Braze (NASDAQ:BRZE) was down during early trading Friday, following its first quarter fiscal 2026 results, but analysts remain optimistic as revenue growth is likely to accelerate in the quarters ahead.

Although Braze, a customer engagement platform, beat revenue and earnings per share estimates in the first quarter, new subscription revenue was less than the market anticipated.

"While sales commentary was positive, the $1mm 1Q Q/Q subscription revenue increase was underwhelming and the lowest since the IPO, but the 2Q step up is in line with historical trends suggesting 1Q was more backend loaded," said Needham analysts, led by Scott Berg, in a Friday investor note. "Overall, we came away optimistic regarding platform technology enhancements that can come from the OfferFit acquisition, which could be accretive to growth in FY27."

During the first quarter, Braze announced it was acquiring OfferFit, an artificial intelligence decisioning company, for $325M. AI decisioning agents can autonomously experiment, learn, and deliver personalized experiences. 

"Focused on the decisioning layer, OfferFit replaces the manual work of A/B testing with reinforcement learning agents that autonomously experiment and learn optimal actions," said OfferFit CEO George Khachatryan, back in March when the acquisition was announced.

The acquisition is expected to close during the second quarter of fiscal 2026.

"While still early days, Braze sounded optimistic regarding the technological capabilities OfferFit will bring to the platform, specifically its distinctive reinforcement learning products and services, which the company believes will allow them to drive overall ACV growth and further strengthen Braze's competitive position, along with benefitting Project Catalyst," Berg said.

Needham reiterated its Buy rating and $50 price target following the results.

Meanwhile, Wells Fargo retained its Overweight rating and $40 price target, noting that Braze modestly increased it guidance.

"BRZE provided updated FY26 guidance of $702-$706M (~18%-19% y/y) and OM of 0.7-1.2% vs prior st ests of $689.1M (~17% y/y) and 4.0%," said Wells Fargo analysts, led by Michael Berg, in an investor note. "Notably this includes ~$11-$12M from OfferFit, implying the co raised top line guide by the amount of the beat, in-line w/bogeys, while the reduction in OM is ~80% from OfferFit and ~20% from BRZE investments to drive integration efforts."

Braze competitors include Salesforce (CRM), Adobe (ADBE) and Klaviyo (KVYO). All three stocks inched up between 1% to 2% during early Friday trading.

data.title: Stock index futures jump as labor report beats consensus
data.description:
Stock index futures jumped on Friday, and yields rose, as the labor report showed slower growth in May but still reported a healthy gain.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.8%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.8%, and Dow futures (INDU) +0.7%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 8 basis points to 4.47%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 7 basis points to 4.01%.

May Nonfarm payrolls: +139K vs. +126K consensus and +147K prior (revised from +177K), according to data released by the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics on Friday.

Unemployment rate: 4.2% vs. 4.2% consensus and 4.2% prior. 

The indication of a potential thaw in tensions between Musk and Trump comes after a public feud that erupted between them on Thursday. Earlier, their clash escalated when the president threatened to terminate Musk’s government contracts, leading to a 14% drop in Tesla’s (TSLA) shares.

Wall Street closed in the red as Tesla (TSLA) shaved off more than $100B in market capitalization amid Trump and Musk's verbal spat. 

"Markets had a volatile session yesterday, as they grappled with a barrage of news that each pushed in different directions. Those included positive U.S.-China headlines amid a call between Trump and Xi, a hawkish ECB decision, and more weak data from the U.S. But the most remarkable was an extraordinary war of words between Trump and Elon Musk that ultimately left risk assets losing ground," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. 

The trade headlines outweighed an initial negative reaction to the latest weekly U.S. jobless claims, which added to fears that the U.S. labor market was finally deteriorating after Liberation Day, Reid added. 

Trump on Thursday said that he had held a "very good phone call" with China's leader Xi Jinping which "resulted in a very positive conclusion" for the two nations.

data.title: RFK Jr. looks to fast track rare disease drug approvals
data.description:
• HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. on Thursday said that the U.S. FDA would examine new methods of fast tracking new medicines for rare diseases to market.
  • He noted this would be done by proving a less cumbersome regulatory framework. 
  • "We are going to continue to figure out new ways of accelerating approvals for drugs and treatments that treat rare diseases, and we're going to make this country the hub of biotechnology innovation," RFK Jr. said at an FDA meeting discuss cell and gene therapies, Reuters reported.
  • Several panel members said that regulations are slowing drug approvals for rare disease treatment, jeopardizing the U.S. position as the world's No.1 center for biotechnology as drug development in China is ramping up.
